title: Baidu Chip Unit Targets $14.7 Billion Valuation In IPO Push
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) is moving to sharpen the investor story around Kunlunxin, its chipmaking unit, as the company pursues an IPO on Shanghai's STAR Board while also keeping a separate Hong Kong listing plan in motion. The unit is working with China International Capital Corp., according to a statement filed by the investment bank to the Chinese Securities Regulatory Commission. Earlier this year, Baidu said Kunlunxin had confidentially filed for an offering in Hong Kong, giving investors another sign that the search giant is trying to carve out the chip business at a time when appetite for China's semiconductor names remains strong. Baidu's Hong Kong shares rose as much as 4.1% on Friday, possibly reflecting the market's reaction to the listing push.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Signs with BIDU. Is BIDU fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The setup could be meaningful because Chinese chipmakers have been drawing heavy investor interest across both Shanghai and Hong Kong. Shares of Shanghai Biren Technology, Metax Integrated Circuits Shanghai and Moore Threads Technology have rallied since their debuts last year, while Shanghai's tech-heavy Star 50 Index has climbed more than 20% this year and reached an all-time high on Thursday. Kunlunxin, in which Baidu holds a 58% stake, was valued at at least $3 billion in a Bloomberg report from December. The valuation bar may now be much higher, with the South China Morning Post reporting Friday that Kunlunxin is seeking at least 100 billion yuan, or $14.7 billion, for its Hong Kong IPO, citing unidentified people.

For investors, the bigger angle is China's continued race to build and finance local semiconductor champions as US restrictions reshape access to advanced chips from companies such as Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA). Baidu started Kunlunxin years ago to support the computing power behind its AI ambitions, and the company said earlier this year that a separate listing could raise the unit's profile and help tap capital markets for additional funding. Jefferies analysts including Thomas Chong said the Shanghai listing plan shows progress in Baidu's carveout strategy because an onshore listing could make it easier for Chinese AI model developers and chipmakers to access domestic investors. Jefferies expects Kunlunxin's Hong Kong listing to happen in the third quarter, while recent model updates from DeepSeek and ByteDance could continue feeding demand for domestic chip suppliers including Huawei Technologies and Cambricon Technologies.

title: Do Lower Near‑Term Estimates Change the Long‑Term Story for Baidu (BIDU)?
content:
In recent days, Baidu Inc. has come under investor scrutiny as earnings estimates for the current quarter and full fiscal year have been revised lower, even as projections for the next fiscal year point to possible improvement. This shift in expectations, combined with Baidu’s current Zacks Rank #4 (Sell), highlights a tension between nearer-term caution and longer-term optimism in analyst sentiment. We will now examine how these earnings estimate revisions and the Zacks Rank shape Baidu’s investment narrative for investors.

Uncover the next big thing with 25 elite penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

What Is Baidu's Investment Narrative?

To own Baidu today, you need to believe that its core search and advertising engine, plus its AI and autonomous driving bets, can justify a premium valuation over time despite uneven profitability. The new dividend policy and multi‑year US$5.00 billion buyback plan suggest management is confident in the company’s cash generation, even as reported margins remain compressed by large one‑off items. International Apollo Go rollouts in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, and tools like DuClaw in Baidu AI Cloud, are key near‑term catalysts that could support sentiment if they start to translate into clearer revenue and earnings contribution. Against that, the recent downward revisions to near‑term earnings estimates and the Zacks Rank #4 (Sell) sharpen the focus on execution risk: investors are now watching more closely for any delay or slippage in monetizing these AI investments.

However, investors should watch how Baidu balances heavy AI spend with still‑thin margins. Baidu's shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 33%. Uncover the fair value now.

Exploring Other PerspectivesBIDU 1-Year Stock Price Chart

The Simply Wall St Community’s 9 fair value views span about US$105.26 to US$240.12 per share, underlining how far opinions can diverge. Set those against near‑term earnings downgrades and Baidu’s rich earnings multiple, and you can see why many participants are weighing AI growth ambitions against margin pressure and execution risk.

title: Alibaba and TSMC Stocks Jump. Three Reasons Asia’s AI Rally Got Another Boost.
content:
In South Korea, fellow chip makers SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics rose 13% and 5.4%, respectively, helping propel the benchmark index to another record high. Many major Asian indexes were closed on Friday for the May Day holiday, meaning investors were playing catch-up—and opted to double down on some of the chip makers and Big Tech names benefiting from the AI boom when they returned. President Donald Trump on Sunday laid out a plan to escort commercial vessels through the waterway, although tensions escalated after Asian markets closed on Monday when Iranian state media reported that two missiles had hit a U.S. frigate.

title: Is Baidu, Inc. (BIDU) The Best Chinese Stock to Buy According to Hedge Funds?
content:
Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is among the best Chinese stocks to buy according to hedge funds. On April 14, Macquarie trimmed the price target on Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) to $158 from $177 and maintained an Outperform rating. If we consider the stock’s performance, it has witnessed a year-to-date decline of nearly 14%. Even then, the firm anticipates AI cloud infrastructure to enhance AI-driven revenue in Q1 of the current fiscal year.

With advertising appearing soft, Macquarie expects advertising revenue to stay under strain in the times ahead. What sets Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) apart as one of the best Chinese stocks to buy is its continuous investments in AI technology and cost optimization measures.

360b / Shutterstock.com

On the same day, BofA Securities reaffirmed a Buy rating on Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU). According to the firm, Baidu Core’s Q1 topline and bottom line will be in line with expectations. With a price target of $180, the firm keeps its first-quarter topline guidance and adjusted operating profit at a 2% and 24% YoY decline, respectively. That said, the firm anticipates revenue growth rebound throughout the second and fourth quarters of FY26.

Baidu, Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is a Beijing-based provider of a range of services, including internet content and internet maps. Incorporated in 2000, the company operates in two segments: Baidu General Business and iQIYI.

While we acknowledge the potential of BIDU as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Baidu Inc. (BIDU) Launches Upgraded General AI Agent
content:
Baidu Inc. (NASDAQ:BIDU) is one of the 10 Best AI Stocks to Watch in May. On April 28, the company announced the launch of GenFlow 4.0, an upgraded version of its general AI agent. Jointly released by Baidu Wenku and Baidu Drive, GenFlow 4.0 comes with a fully revamped Office Agent at its core, which allows users to invoke PowerPoint, Excel, and Word Agents in parallel from a single prompt.

Additionally, the company said the AI agent is also deeply integrated with OpenClaw, which can be deployed in one click from the Baidu Drive PC or mobile app, turning Baidu Drive into a personal AI workspace.Baidu’s (BIDU) New AI Tool Turns Images into Videos — Here’s What You Need to Know

Gil C / Shutterstock.com

In the past year, Baidu’s stock surged 44.08%, while it posted a 15.82% year-to-date decline. Based on 37 analyst ratings compiled by CNN, the Baidu stock has an average price target of $179.55, a 41.90% upside from the current price of $125.76.

On April 1, Baidu announced that, in partnership with the Roads and Transport Authority (RTA), it has launched a fully driverless commercial ride-hailing service in Dubai via the Apollo Go app, marking the platform’s first international app deployment and a major milestone in the region. The company said the launch was done through Apollo Go’s strategic collaboration with Dubai Taxi Company (DTC), the emirate’s leading mobility service provider, bringing strong local operational expertise to support the service.

Baidu said Dubai marks a key strategic hub in Apollo Go’s growing global network, as it continues to expand its footprint worldwide. As of February 2026, Apollo Go has completed over 20 million rides worldwide, with weekly rides peaking at more than 300,000 during Q4 2025.

Baidu is a leading AI company with a strong internet foundation. It offers a full AI stack of four layers, including cloud infrastructure, an in-house developed deep learning framework, PaddlePaddle, self-developed ERNIE foundation models, and applications.

While we acknowledge the potential of BIDU as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk.
